item.title: I’m down in the dumps
item.description: My doggy suddenly passed away last week due to a tumor on her spleen exploding. I’m moving across the country in a week without her. The fifth year of my grandfather’s (more like a father to me) passing is tomorrow. This month is trash. This year is trash. The past five years were trash.

item.title: The more you know
item.dc:creator: WeirdAlisMySpiritUserName

item.title: Rest easy, my sweet boy.
item.description:
I know selfies aren’t allowed, but this is my favorite photo with my sweet boy and I just need to throw my sadness into the ether.
The folks who have followed along probably know about my former dog, Seymour (left). He passed on my birthday at the beginning of September last year. He had an undetected tumor on his heart and went from my healthy, athletic boy to death’s door overnight. I felt like I died with him and I’ve spent the last year trudging and slashing through the most debilitating grief. Duke (right) is technically not my dog, but I have taken care of him for 7 out of his 10 years when his human works and travels.
Duke and Seymour were more than just best friends. The relationship they shared is one of the greatest treasures I’ll carry through the rest of my life. These boys fucking loved each other. And I fucking love them. They were my whole world.
I wouldn’t believe in soulmates if it wasn’t for Seymour. We were each other’s. I now believe we can have multiple soulmates in this life and they can come in all forms. I think Duke and Seymour also shared a soul bond. And I think that Duke and I shared a soul bond too, even if it wasn’t as much as me and Seymour.
Both of these dogs had a way of looking so deeply beyond your eyes, like they were looking into you. Like they could see your skeleton and everything you couldn’t say. To look back into their’s was like looking into an infinite universe of love and wonder, speckled with stars and colors that don’t have names. The whole world would just fall away into quiet with these guys. Nothing else mattered.
We had over 6 years of adventures together.
I look back on photos like these and I’ve never had such an urge to travel back through time to be back in this space.
These pictures speak for themselves. I can’t even conjure words adequate enough to describe their friendship and bond. I don’t know that language exists to quantify it.
Always near each other.
Always full of love.
Always taking it in together.
One of my favorite photos from the same day as the first. The third dog is Alice/Flower. She belongs to my former landlord and is missing an eye and a couple toes. She survived yeeting herself out of a third story window and didn’t get so much as a scratch.
Sorry, but I love this photo too.
Duke wasn’t allowed on furniture at home, but if mom was on vacation, then dang it, so was he. So I allowed it at my house.
Asking to come up on the bed.
My sweet, gentle giant.
Seymour was a weirdo who loved to lick Duke’s toes. Duke hated it, but loved his friend too much to stop him, so he just let him do it.
Always cheesin’
Love love love.
Cruisin’
Cuddlin’
I feel like this is a representation of them now. Duke joined Seymour wherever good dogs go when they have to say goodbye to their people. Duke was with me for his final days. His human dropped him off with me on Thursday because she had to go out of town for the weekend. She said she was surprised, because she always asks him if he wants to go to Bri’s (me) and he always gets right up and goes to the door and waits to go out to the car. This time, he refused to get up and didn’t eat. He also pooped in the house. She said she was surprised and worried and for me to call if anything was off with him. I can’t explain it, but something in my gut was so heavy and off. I can’t say I knew this was his time, but I just felt this was our last time together. I know that sounds stupid, but I just did and I don’t need anyone to understand. I knew the risk I was accepting by taking a dog as old and big as him. But I love him and I knew we only had so many visits left.
I hope they are now running pain free together just like this. Just like cancer took my Seymour, it also took my Duke. He woke up Friday and I knew something was wrong. He has been sore and tired for a few months. But this was different. I knew this was the beginning of the end. He rapidly declined throughout the day and I stayed up all night with him last night. His mom couldn’t get back til the next morning (this morning), so I just held him and tried not to cry. I told him I loved him so much. I told him how grateful I was that he was Seymour’s best friend and was always so kind to him and to my kitty who was always a bully to him. I told him how grateful I was that he loved me. How grateful I was that he comforted me when Seymour died. His pain meds did nothing and my poor boy just cried in my arms. The EV was at capacity and couldn’t see us til the morning when his mom was coming back. I couldn’t lift him into my car alone anyway. I just tried to make him as comfortable and loved as possible.
This was from his 9th birthday. He got a pup cake.
I tried to get a video prior to this. They were both on their sides, gently pawing each other’s face. Almost like they were trying to rub each other’s cheek. As soon as I tried to film it, Seymour got up.
Being silly.
That face.
I don’t know how the bed frame withstood him.
Always holding paws.
Trumpet and tuba.
Duke made Seymour feel safe.
Taking vacation too far, but @TheJazzButcher is even more lenient than I am.
Tonight, my heart is broken for his loss. And the scar of my grief for Seymour feels freshly reopened. Duke helped me feel close to Seymour and losing him also feels like losing Seymour again. It’s a double whammy. I just wanted to put all these photos somewhere and write out some feelings. Me and Jazz have lost so many of our beloved creatures, his grandparents, and a good friend over the past year. It sometimes blows my mind how much death has been the theme of our lives lately. I am a death positive person, and navigating grief is still a profoundly painful journey. My only peace tonight is that my boys are together again. I don’t necessarily believe in heaven, but I like to think there is a special place that good dogs go when they have to leave their bodies and us behind. I like to think that soul bonds like that don’t die with a body. I love you, my sweet boys. Take care of each other.
item.dc:creator: AtlasHuggedrescuepup

item.title: Karma
item.description:
I'm a walking deterrent to workplace food thieves. So... I love spicy food. I don't mean Taco Bell Spicy. I don't mean "Wendy's Ghost Pepper Fries" spicy. I mean when I pack a lunch and put it in the fridge at work, it's got a minimum of one carolina reaper mixed into it, and that's if I'm feeling like mild food that day. So a number of years ago I go a job as a security guard. I made myself some mapodoufu, and I used carolina reaper as the sole type of pepper used to spice it up. The batch had two whole peppers in it. Several hours after I started my shift, there was a horrible screaming sound coming from the workplace break room. One of the other guards had gotten into my mapodoufu. Dude didn't even know what the food was, but he'd put it in the microwave to reheat it and took a bite. Needless to say everybody stayed away from that container but me the rest of the day. A few sat there and watched me eat the whole thing without drinking anything before finishing my lunch break. Next day, I make some homemade boneless wings... with homemade mango carolina reaper sauce. Some other idiot thought it was orange chicken. Second verse same as the first and a little bit worse. Next day I brought a homemade bean, beef, and cheese burrito. With homemade carolina reaper salsa. Rinse and repeat the previous days. That day I was told to start labeling my food with my name. Next day everybody labeled their food with my name and nobody ate food they didn't place in the fridge themselves.
My carolina reaper mango ketchup. It's basically ketchup made with mangos instead of tomatoes, and with carolina reapers thrown into the pot while it boiled down into a mush.
Chimkin nuggies with my reaper mango ketchup.
I sometimes give bottles of the stuff to friends as a gift. The heat topped bottles are the ones I give out. I always pour the rest into my beer bottles for personal use. If you think I just ripped the label off existing hot sauce, here's the hot sauce bottles I used in the photo. I don't make any money off this link, just showing you where I got them from. https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B08V925X96 I used a heat gun to do the shrink wrap part. This is the swing top beer bottle I use for the stuff I use myself. https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B08GG9PJ68
Here's some more. I make loads of this stuff. Each of the 5 oz bottles has 1.5 carolina reapers in it.
here it is in a pot, simmering. There's also caramelized vidalia onion in there.
Oh and the vinegar I use is mango wine vinegar. Not mango infused wine, wine made from mango juice as opposed to grape must. The result is simultaneously the HOTTEST sauce you've ever had and the tastiest, assuming you're like me and you can take the heat. If you can't take the heat, it's literally just pain. Thick, viscous, liquid pain. So I use fresh mango fruit in place of tomato pulp, a bit of mango powder, roasted garlic, caramelized vidalia onion, mango vingar, sugar, and a bit of pink himalayan salt because but I'm not particular it's just what I get because it looks pretty in my glass jar salt grinder. I don't really have a recipe, I just eyeball it for every batch. The number of reapers per 5 ounce bottle is a minimum of 1 and a half, but sometimes can be as much as 2 per bottle.

item.title: Some programming humor for a niche audience
item.description:
1
2. Now that is what I call HARDCORE GAMING
3. The IT girl
4
5. Is this punishment enough?
6.
7
8
9
10. FUCKIN TRUE
11
12. Is this guy an ATM speedrunner or what?
13. This might be a trap
14. A simple guide for anyone to follow
15. And finally I would like to share something from myself: I've been working very hard on a ambitious Stanley Parable-inspired game with a touch of GladOS from Portal called Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse). The demo made to TOP 10 on Steam's demo trending tab! And it features witty signs like this one here. "What?", I hear you ask. "I can read funny signs for free?" YES, yes you can! Try it now on Steam and please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse Thank you, and if this dump made you smile at least once, I am happy with it! : )

item.title: Michelle Jenneke Has Made the Australian Track & Field Team for Paris 2024, 100m hurdles
item.description: Jenneke will begin competition at the 2024 Paris Games with the first round of the women's 100m hurdles on Wednesday, August 7. The final is slated for Saturday, August 10. https://www.si.com/onsi/athlete-lifestyle/news/michelle-jenneke-dance-australia-track-olympics-photos-video
